label: Chroma
description: Connect to a Chroma vector database — self-hosted or Chroma Cloud — to store and query embeddings.

form.title: Connect Chroma
form.subtitle: Point Anton at your Chroma server or Chroma Cloud tenant to read and write vector collections.

methods.label: Self-hosted Chroma server
methods.description: Paste the URL of your locally or privately hosted Chroma instance. No API token required for unauthenticated servers; add a static token if you enabled token auth.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to a self-hosted Chroma server

### Prerequisites

- A running Chroma server (`chroma run` or Docker). Default port is `8000`.
- If you enabled token authentication (`CHROMA_SERVER_AUTH_PROVIDER`), have your static token ready.

### Steps

1. Find the address of your Chroma server, e.g. `http://localhost:8000` or `http://192.168.1.50:8000`.
2. Paste it into **Server URL** below (include the scheme and port).
3. If your server was started with `CHROMA_SERVER_AUTH_CREDENTIALS=<token>`, paste that value into **API Token**. Leave it blank for unauthenticated servers.
4. Optionally set a **Tenant** and **Database** name if your deployment uses multi-tenancy (defaults are `default_tenant` / `default_database`).
5. Click **Submit** — Anton will issue a `/api/v1/heartbeat` check to verify connectivity.

### Notes

- If Chroma runs inside Docker and Anton runs on the host, use `http://host.docker.internal:8000` instead of `localhost`.
- TLS-terminated servers should use `https://`.
- Token auth is set server-side via environment variables; see the [Chroma auth docs](https://docs.trychroma.com/usage-guide#authentication) for details.

fields.name: server_url
fields.label: Server URL
fields.description: Full URL of your Chroma server, including scheme and port.

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: API Token
fields.description: Static bearer token if you enabled token authentication on the server. Leave blank for open servers.

fields.name: tenant
fields.label: Tenant
fields.description: Chroma tenant name. Leave as default unless you use multi-tenancy.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: Chroma database name within the tenant.

methods.label: Chroma Cloud
methods.description: Connect to a managed Chroma Cloud tenant using your Cloud API key, tenant ID, and database name from the Chroma Cloud dashboard.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to Chroma Cloud

### Prerequisites

- A Chroma Cloud account at [trychroma.com](https://trychroma.com).
- A database created in the Chroma Cloud dashboard.

### Steps

1. Log in to [Chroma Cloud](https://cloud.trychroma.com).
2. Navigate to **Settings → API Keys** and click **Create API Key**. Copy the key — it is shown only once.
3. From the dashboard home, copy your **Tenant ID** (shown under your account name or in the URL).
4. Open the database you want Anton to use and copy its **Database Name**.
5. Paste all three values into the fields below.
6. The **Server URL** defaults to `https://api.trychroma.com` — only change it if Chroma Cloud directs you to a regional endpoint.
7. Click **Submit** to verify the connection.

### Notes

- API keys are scoped to your tenant. Rotate them in **Settings → API Keys** if compromised.
- Each database within a tenant is isolated; Anton will only access the database you specify.

fields.name: server_url
fields.label: Server URL
fields.description: Chroma Cloud API endpoint. Use the default unless directed to a regional URL.

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: API key from Chroma Cloud → Settings → API Keys. Shown only once at creation.

fields.name: tenant
fields.label: Tenant ID
fields.description: Your Chroma Cloud tenant ID, found in the dashboard header or URL.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database Name
fields.description: The name of the Chroma Cloud database Anton should connect to.
